questions.question: What is the topic for Week 31?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-One is SELF-WORTH.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 30?
questions.explanation: Crisis Management was the topic for Week 30.

questions.question: I will praise You, for I am fearfully and ________ made.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Psalm 139:14) says 'for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Psalm 139:14.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 31 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: I will praise You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Marvelous are Your works, And that my soul knows very well.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Psalm 139:14.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Psalm 139:14), what does the psalmist's soul know very well?
options: A. That the earth is round
B. That Marvelous are God's works
D. That tomorrow is not promised
questions.explanation: The verse says 'Marvelous are Your works, And that my soul knows very well.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is self-worth?
options: A. The amount of money in your bank account
B. The core beliefs you have about your values
C. How many friends you have
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'Self-worth connotes the core beliefs you have about your values.'

questions.question: According to the lesson, self-worth is usually temporary and changes every day.
questions.explanation: The text states 'Self-worth is relatively stable or permanent such that it becomes an enduring determinant of several decisions later in life.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'UNDERSTAND SELF-WORTH'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what are the three major components of self-worth?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'self-awareness, self-acceptance and self-love'.

questions.question: Which component of self-worth involves understanding that making mistakes and experiencing setbacks is part of life and being human?
questions.explanation: Under Self-acceptance, the text says 'We understand that making mistakes, experiencing setbacks... is part of life and being human.'

questions.question: Which component refers to the ability to recognise our emotional triggers, habits, strengths, and weaknesses?
questions.explanation: Under Self-awareness, the text says 'Self-awareness refers to the ability to recognise our emotional triggers and habits. It also involves identifying strengths and weaknesses.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding self-acceptance removing anxiety and making us feel confident?
questions.explanation: The text says 'This removes anxiety and can make us feel confident and secure (Philippians 4:6; Matthew 6:27).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'OVERCOME NEGATIVE SELF-PERCEPTION'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, where does negative self-perception often begin?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Negative self-perception often begins in the mind (Proverbs 4:23, 2 Corinthians 10:5).'

questions.question: What does the lesson say breeds discontent and insecurity?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Avoid comparing yourself to others, as comparison breeds discontent and insecurity (2 Corinthians 10:12).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, we should live our lives relying on human opinion or approval.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'Do not live your life relying on human opinion or approval because our worth is based on God’s approval...'

questions.question: How is God's power made perfect in us, according to the lesson?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Accept God’s grace, knowing that His power is made perfect in your weaknesses (2 Corinthians 12:9).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support having an attitude of gratitude to shift focus away from negative thoughts?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(1 Thessalonians 5:18)' next to the attitude of gratitude.

questions.question: According to the conclusion, what is essential for living a confident and fulfilled life in Christ?
questions.explanation: The conclusion states 'Understanding self-worth is essential for living a confident and fulfilled life in Christ.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'And Saul said to David, “You are not able to go against this Philistine to fight with him; for you are a youth, and he a man of war from his youth.”'
questions.verse_text: And Saul said to David, “You are not able to go against this Philistine to fight with him; for you are a youth, and he a man of war from his youth.”
questions.explanation: This is verse 33 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (1 Samuel 17:33), why did Saul say David could not fight the Philistine?
options: B. Because David was a youth, and the Philistine a man of war from his youth
C. Because David had no armor
D. Because David was a shepherd
questions.explanation: Verse 33 says '...for you are a youth, and he a man of war from his youth.'

questions.question: In 1 Samuel 17:34, what did David say he used to keep for his father?
questions.explanation: Verse 34 says 'Your servant used to keep his father’s sheep...'

questions.question: According to 1 Samuel 17:35, what did David do when the lion or bear arose against him?
options: C. He caught it by its beard, and struck and killed it
D. He hid in a cave
questions.explanation: Verse 35 says 'and when it arose against me, I caught it by its beard, and struck and killed it.'

questions.question: In 1 Samuel 17:36, David said the Philistine will be like one of the lion or bear because the Philistine had done what?
options: B. Defied the armies of the living God
questions.explanation: Verse 36 says 'seeing he has defied the armies of the living God.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that God values each of us deeply and considers us precious?
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'God values each of us deeply and considers us precious (Isaiah 43:4; John 3:16).'

questions.question: Self-love pushes us to deliberately make an effort at eating healthy, taking good care of ourselves, and letting go of toxic relationships.
questions.explanation: This is exactly what is described under the 'Self-love' component in Outline I.

questions.question: Speak words of life and affirmation over yourself, aligning your speech with God's ________.
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'aligning your speech with God’s truth (Proverbs 18:21).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding letting go of past mistakes and embracing the new identity you have in Christ?
questions.explanation: Outline II cites '(Philippians 3:13-14)' for letting go of past mistakes.

questions.question: Our value comes from knowing that God created us in His image and likeness.
questions.explanation: Outline I begins with 'Our value comes from knowing that God created us in His image and likeness (Genesis 1:26-27).'
